payload.title: MTA Board Action Items
payload.description: MTA Board meeting packet including joint board meeting minutes from April 30, 2024, staff summaries for procurements, fare promotions, investment report, and other board action items.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA Grand Central Madison Operating Company (GCMC)

payload.entity_name: MTA Grand Central Madison Operating Company
payload.description: Created to oversee operation and maintenance of Grand Central Madison Concourse and related facilities.
Vendor providing the New Fare Payment System (OMNY) for MTA, headquartered in San Diego, CA.
Vendor to replace and upgrade fare collection solution (TVMs/TOMs) for LIRR and Metro-North Railroad, headquartered in Lowell, MA.
Vendor administering the Mobile Ticketing Program (TrainTime) for LIRR and Metro-North Railroad, headquartered in New York, New York.

Phase 3 of the E-Hail premium service pilot program for Access-A-Ride paratransit eligible customers with disabilities, raising per-trip subsidy cap to $60 and sorting customers into 25 or 40 subsidized trips per month groups.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison Operations and Maintenance Contract

payload.project_name: Grand Central Madison Operations and Maintenance Contract
payload.description: Contract to George S. Hall, Inc. for operation and maintenance of Grand Central Madison Concourse, associated ventilation/power facilities, and communication rooms in Sunnyside Yards, Queens.

payload.project_name: Mobile Ticketing Program for LIRR and Metro-North Railroad
payload.description: Noncompetitive contract to Masabi LLC to continue administering the Mobile Ticketing Program (TrainTime) for LIRR and Metro-North Railroad with OMNY integration.

payload.project_name: Fare Collection System Replacement for LIRR and MNR
payload.description: Noncompetitive contract to Scheidt & Bachmann USA to replace and upgrade fare collection solution including Ticket Vending Machines/Ticket Office Machines for LIRR and Metro-North Railroad.

payload.project_name: MTA 2024 Temporary Fare Promotions: Connecting Services and Summer Saturdays
payload.description: Temporary fare pilot promotions including discount on connecting services for Orange/Rockland County customers, summer weekend ferry service on Haverstraw-Ossining Ferry, parking discount at North White Plains, and Summer Saturdays program for LIRR and MNR.

Ratification of awards to Hayden AI ($83,133,058 for up to 1,023 ABLE systems) and Seon Corp ($58,405,780 for up to 1,000 ABLE systems) for purchase, installation, operation, and maintenance of ABLE systems.

tool_args.label: OMNY New Fare Payment System Modification

payload.project_name: New Fare Payment System (OMNY) Contract Modification
payload.description: Modification to Cubic contract A34024 to remove LIRR/MNR requirements, add B2B Portal and mobile app enhancements, and extend schedule to December 2025.
E-Hail Phase 3 begins with transition of current Phase 2 customers to one of the two Phase 3 programs.
Congestion pricing toll collection planned to commence. First people to pay the toll will pay $3.75 (overnight period rate).
Summer Saturdays program on LIRR and MNR allowing monthly pass holders to travel with up to two additional people for $1 per person.
New customers begin to be added to Phase 3 E-Hail program pending budget analysis.
MTA will evaluate budget impacts and customer response to Phase 3 program; if positive, will invite up to 800 additional customers.
First phase of the E-Hail pilot ran from October 2017 through August 2023, included 1,200 AAR customers.
MTA restructured E-Hail program and began Phase 2 with limits on rides per customer and subsidy amounts.
Annualized cost of Phase 2 of the E-Hail Program at the end of its first year, in August 2024

payload.raw_value_text: an additional $2 million annually for up to 800 additional enrollees
payload.description: Phase 3 projected additional annual cost for up to 800 additional enrollees
Customer satisfaction with the Phase 2 pilot based on a survey conducted in January 2024

payload.scope: E-Hail pilot customers across Distance and High Volume programs

payload.raw_value_text: reduced E-Hail costs by 80% per participant compared to Phase 1
payload.description: Setting monthly trip and subsidy allocations reduced E-Hail costs by 80% per participant compared to Phase 1
Negotiated credit to MTA from modification to Cubic Contract A34024 for New Fare Payment System
Credit from deleting remaining Railroad Work from Cubic contract for New Fare Payment System
Reduction in operating costs from deleting Railroad Work from Cubic contract

payload.raw_value_text: $2,100,000 ($1,900,000 in capital costs and $200,000 in operating costs)

Customer satisfaction with paratransit system under Chris Pangilinan's leadership

payload.claim_text: E-Hail is a premium service for people with disabilities eligible for the MTA's Access-A-Ride paratransit program. It provides more flexibility than dedicated or non-dedicated (i.e. broker) Access-A-Ride service by enabling customers to book taxi or for-hire vehicle (FHV) rides directly.
payload.subject: E-Hail program

tool_args.label: Masabi Software Critical to Fare Operations

payload.claim_text: Since the 2016 launch of mobile ticketing, Masabi's software has become critical to Railroad fare collection operations.
payload.subject: Masabi mobile ticketing software

tool_args.label: Railroad Fare Systems Past Service Life

payload.claim_text: The current fare collection systems in use by the Railroads were installed almost 25 years ago and are well-past their expected service life. These systems cannot be maintained in a state of good repair beyond 2025.
payload.subject: LIRR and MNR fare collection systems

tool_args.label: S&B TVMs/TOMs Aging and PCI Compliance

payload.claim_text: The current fare collection TVMs/TOMs were first installed by S&B in year 2000. Over time, hardware components have been refurbished and upgraded multiple times to keep these obsolete ticketing machines operational. By year 2025, S&B will be unable to maintain the same level of support. TVMs/TOMs represent 30 percent of the Railroads' ticket sales with 99 percent availability.
payload.subject: Scheidt & Bachmann TVMs/TOMs

payload.claim_text: By charging vehicles a toll to enter the Congestion Relief Zone, defined as local streets and avenues in Manhattan at or below 60th Street, Congestion Pricing will encourage drivers to switch to transit (subways, buses, and railroads) into the Congestion Relief Zone and avoid the toll.
payload.subject: Congestion Pricing

payload.claim_text: Substantial Completion of the Contract can be achieved by December 2025, with better functionality and a substantial savings in delay costs.
payload.subject: Cubic OMNY Contract

payload.claim_text: MTA and TBTA currently have $3.73 billion of outstanding Build America Bonds ('BABs'). The 35% cash subsidy payments on the BABs have been significantly reduced since 2013 through federal budget sequestration increasing the net effective interest cost of the taxable BABs.
payload.subject: Build America Bonds
